sections.title: A: Write one word in each gap
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.
sections.passage:
Dear Sir,

I would like to (1) ...... a complaint about an article that appeared in your newspaper last week. In the article, entitled 'Why they are (2) ...... of work', the writer suggested that the unemployed were responsible (3) ...... their own situation. If the writer had (4) ...... their job properly and (5) ...... a little effort to speak to local unemployed people, they might have realised that this is not the case.

Let me begin (6) ...... saying that most unemployed people want to work. They apply (7) ...... jobs and (8) ...... a great deal of effort into changing their situation. The truth is that local businesses are simply not taking (9) ...... new staff. The government is to blame for the way in which economic growth has (10) ...... down, making life difficult for many businesses.

I look forward to reading a more balanced article in the future.

Yours faithfully,
Rebecca Winterson

sections.title: B: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals
sections.instructions: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals when this is necessary.

items.sentence: Dave left the office keys in a pub and he was sacked for being so (RESPONSIBLE) .......
'Leave it to me. I'll find a solution,' the manager said, (HELP) .......
All (APPLY) ...... are asked to send their CV to Mrs Simpson in the Personnel Department.
My job is to (SUPERVISE) ...... the telesales workers.
Carol has worked for us for over twenty years with great loyalty and (DEDICATE) .......
All our (EMPLOY) ...... receive four weeks paid holiday per year.
The managing director didn't think the suggestion would ever be (WORK) ......, so we had to think of another solution.
It seems that Mr Jones lied about his (QUALIFY) ...... on his application form.

sections.title: C: Complete the second sentence using the word given
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.

items.sentence:
Yuri qualified and then immediately found a really good job. **than**
No ...... he found a really good job.

Women don't often get promoted in some industries. **do**
Rarely ...... in some industries.

I never realised that Tony was the head of Fizzco. **realise**
Little ...... that Tony was the head of Fizzco.

I didn't know about the meeting until Tracy rang. **did**
Not until ...... about the meeting.

I think you will benefit from getting a new job. **good**
I think it will be ...... a new job.

The two companies finally agreed the deal. **reached**
The two companies finally ...... the deal.

Customers of the bank are never allowed into the basement area. **circumstances**
Under ...... customers of the bank allowed into the basement area.

I didn't think of a good answer to the interviewer's question until later. **did**
Only ...... a good answer to the interviewer's question.

It was such a boring job that no one wanted to do it. **was**
Such ...... that no one wanted to do it.
Ironworks Limited has closed **D** down and 400 people have lost their jobs.
items.answer: D – down and 400 people have lost their jobs.

items.sentence: Henry asked Janet to stand **A** in for him while he went into town.
items.answer: A – in for him while he went into town.

items.sentence: Mr Carter asked me to see **F** to the orders while he spoke to a customer.
items.answer: F – to the orders while he spoke to a customer.

items.sentence: I heard that Bigburger have taken **C** over Cookright and plan to close some branches.
items.answer: C – over Cookright and plan to close some branches.

items.sentence: Roger says he never set **G** out to become a millionaire - it just happened!
items.answer: G – out to become a millionaire - it just happened!

items.sentence: Apparently, Fatfoods are bringing **B** out a new flavour of ice cream next month.
items.answer: B – out a new flavour of ice cream next month.

items.sentence: I'm thinking of setting **E** up a business selling my own jewellery.
items.answer: E – up a business selling my own jewellery.

items.sentence: The ...... for this position starts at thirty thousand euros per year.
After working at the same factory for thirty years, my grandfather was looking forward to his .......
Some people ...... to London every day from as far away as Leeds.
Most governments tax people on the amount they ...... each year.
Shelley disagreed with the board's decision and so she ...... and went to work for another company.
When the factory closed, over a hundred people were ...... redundant.
Here at Weatherby's, we provide all our ......, from senior management down, with regular training.
